mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through a lower discount rate, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts coincide with weaker forward returns rather than stronger ones.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines versus non-decline months, or if the sign reverses in a rolling 3-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening labor demand and an earnings-growth slowdown that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This effect should appear when unemployment is clearly accelerating, not when it is flat.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 60% in triggered windows over any 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in PAYEMS and RSAFS identifies broad demand resilience: payroll gains support income while retail sales confirm spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The effect is strongest when both series improve together, not individually.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-payroll-and-retail-strength state does not outperform either single-series state, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in the top-quartile joint-strength episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals improving industrial output and a stronger supply side, which supports cyclical earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when industrial production rises only because of temporary inventory restocking without broader activity improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not higher after positive INDPRO trend changes, or if the effect disappears after controlling for broad market drawdowns in a rolling 4-year test.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its prior trend reflects housing demand weakening, which compresses wealth effects and consumer confidence and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is specific to sharp housing deterioration rather than routine volatility.
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive or neutral 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the average post-trigger return is not below unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation falls while FEDFUNDS stays elevated, real policy tightness eases and equity valuations expand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism depends on disinflation occurring without an offsetting policy easing already priced in.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation declines while FEDFUNDS remains above 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-plus-high-rate episodes do not outperform other inflation regimes on 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition has no incremental power over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS growth combined with low UNRATE indicates robust hiring and tight labor utilization, supporting revenue expectations and near-term S&P 500 gains over the next 5 trading days. The signal is falsified if payroll growth is absorbed by rising unemployment or if labor strength does not translate into positive short-horizon returns.
failure_condition: Reject if positive payroll changes with stable or falling UNRATE fail to produce above-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term loses significance in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A coordinated slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS marks broad final-demand weakening, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down. The signal is stronger when both demand measures decelerate together rather than separately.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth both decelerate versus prior period
failure_condition: Reject if joint deceleration episodes do not lead to materially lower 60-day SPX returns than single-variable deceleration episodes, or if forward returns remain positive in most triggered cases.
